data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats self-consistency iff the critic's question-conditional net signal is positive AND the majority is fragile: E[P(SC_right | C)-P(SC_right | !C)] > 0 with P(fragile) > 0, where fragile = no strict candidate majority. No fixed (e, phi, psi, k) regime is sufficient because the alignment between critic-confidence and correctness is question-conditional.", "mechanism": "SC fails on fragile-majority questions (ties or slim margins resolved by the wrong candidate). Harness wins only if the critic flags the slim-margin wrong candidate AND spares the right alternative, converting a loss to a win. With phi and psi averaged over all questions, two critics with identical (phi, psi) can have opposite net effect: one aligns with correctness (high precision on wrong-majority questions, high recall on right-majority questions), yielding net gain; the other anti-aligns, yielding net loss. The measured 0/0 net in hard-set v4-pro is explained: base error 0.01 means P(fragile) ~ 0, so E[net] = 0 regardless of critic quality. The measured 0/0 net in easy-set v4-flash is explained: critic precision 0.333 means E[net | fragile] ~ -0.34, but P(fragile) ~ 0.04, so E[net] ~ -0.014, indistinguishable from 0 at n=24.", "scope": {"covers": ["regimes where P(fragile) is in (0.05, 0.50) and critic positive-class precision exceeds 0.5 on fragile-majority questions", "k >= 5", "critic confidence and correctness are positively correlated on fragile questions"], "excludes": ["regimes where base error < 1/(k+1) (majority always robust, P(fragile) ~ 0)", "regimes where base error > 0.5 (majority always wrong, filtering cannot rescue)", "critic decisions independent of correctness (random critic)"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Experiment shows harness == SC at every measured P(fragile) in (0.1, 0.4) with critic precision-on-fragile > 0.6 and recall-on-fragile > 0.5, refuting the necessity of positive question-conditional net signal", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment shows harness < SC by >= 0.05 accuracy at P(fragile) = 0.2 with critic precision-on-fragile = 0.7, refuting the sufficiency of the proposed condition and confirming false-flag erosion dominates", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment shows harness > SC by >= 0.03 at P(fragile) = 0.1 with critic precision-on-fragile = 0.4 < 0.5, refuting the precision > 0.5 threshold in the claim", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "This conjecture synthesizes c9386a6c (question-conditional alignment is the determinant) and 862de34b (majority-fragile mid-zone is necessary), adding the claim that both conditions are jointly necessary and sufficient. The experiment must measure P(fragile), critic precision/recall ON fragile-majority questions specifically (not pooled), and net harness-minus-SC per question, then check whether the sign of net correlates with the sign of question-conditional critic signal. Token budget: 60 questions x 3 difficulty tiers x 5 candidates x 2 arms x ~2k tokens/candidate = ~1.8M... too large. Shrink to 45 questions x 3 tiers x 5 candidates x 2 arms x ~2k = ~900k. Still over 300k. Final design: 40 questions x 2 tiers (mid-error ~0.15, high-error ~0.35) x 5 candidates x 2 arms (SC, harness) x ~1.5k tokens avg = ~600k. Need to cut to 20 questions x 2 tiers x 5 candidates x 2 arms x ~1.5k = ~300k. Borderline. Alternative: 30 questions x 2 tiers x 5 candidates x 2 arms x ~1k = ~300k. Feasible with concise prompting."}
